I do not need any explanation after this.
The lateral pharyngeal (parapharyngeal) space shares a continuous fascial plane with the retropharyngeal space along the posterior aspect of the pharynx, allowing fluid or infectious spread due to their close adjacency. However, no direct communication exists between them through a shared passage—this distinguishes them despite being intimately positioned. Therefore, I agree with the original conclusion that the lateral pharyngeal space lacks a direct connection to the sublingual space, reinforcing option B as correct.
